cases.title: Expediting the ALGO Dashboard Lifecycle
cases.problem:
The objective was to design a high-fidelity monitoring tool capable of processing real-time metrics and compliance alerts without overwhelming the operator. Key challenges included:

* Information Density: Organizing "Summary Digests" that incorporate EOD status, compliance breaches, and portfolio performance.
* Delivery Velocity: Reducing the gap between technical requirement gathering and the delivery of functional prototypes.
* Complexity Management: Navigating multi-step operational flows while identifying and solving user pain points.
cases.process:
I implemented a 7-step "AI-Augmented" workflow where artificial intelligence acted as a strategic co-pilot at critical milestones:

* Data Synthesis (Step 2): Leveraged Copilot Researcher to synthesize meeting transcripts and technical content, allowing for rapid feature prioritization.
* Rapid Ideation (Step 3): Utilized a Prompt Coach to engineer effective instructions for Figma Make, generating high-fidelity initial concepts near-instantaneously.
* Iterative Refinement (Step 6): Used Figma Make for continuous design updates, incorporating detailed specifications and evolving insights into the layout.
* Final Integration (Step 7): Manually integrated all AI-generated outputs and user feedback into a cohesive, final concept.
cases.outcome:
The ALGO Dashboard project followed a structured, user-centered approach to centralize complex algorithmic operations and asset management. By integrating AI tools strategically across the design lifecycle, I transformed technical insights and high-density data into a production-ready "Gold Copy" concept with unprecedented speed and precision.

* "Gold Copy" Concept: Delivered the final "EDD Gold Copy," a unified dashboard framework that integrates flow analysis and technical specifications.
* Operational Efficiency: Established a workflow that utilizes AI for speed and clarity while maintaining human-led creative direction.
* Actionable Analytics: Designed specialized views for trading, portfolio rebalancing, and active monitoring to optimize high-stakes decision-making.

metrics.label: Faster design iteration with AI workflow

cases.title: Understanding the role of personal banking
cases.problem: While revamping the transactional portal, we acknowledged the persistent need to provide a swift and adaptable mobile solution to meet the ever-growing demand. Therefore, we aimed to discern the role the banks mobile application should play in our clients lives.
cases.process: The project kicked off with a Discovery Phase focused on understanding user behavior, business priorities, and backend readiness. Through stakeholder interviews, user archetypes, journey mapping, workshops, and benchmarking, we identified a core opportunity: leveraging conversational interfaces and mobile capabilities to make the app the preferred channel for banking users. Our hypothesis was that a chat-based, simple messaging experience would reduce friction and create a more seamless, efficient interaction model. From this research, we defined and proposed a roadmap balancing three critical dimensions — infrastructure readiness, user needs, and business priorities. In the Design Delivery and Testing Phase, we translated insights into a scalable experience system. We ran concept testing and MVP definition cycles, iterating on copies, voice, and tone in close collaboration with the development team. Validation included usability testing, heuristic evaluations, card sorting, user interviews, and hard data from NPS surveys and analytics. The result was a refined, tested design grounded in real user feedback and measurable behavior data.
cases.outcome: We successfully revamped the app and portal, enhancing brand design and recognition in the process. Additionally, we established design principles and systems to ensure cohesion across the organization, resulting in user-friendly services that meet and exceed expectations.

cases.cardTitle: Redefining the way the world refuels
cases.title: Redefining the way the world refuels
cases.problem: The EV industry is changing the way humans move, and Blink is changing the way we refuel — by turning businesses into hubs for vehicle charging. We were hired to create a website that serves as a cohesive digital brochure, lead generating tool, and customer resource across Blink's global footprint.
cases.process:
Our approach centered on three pillars: elevating the way users and search engines interact with the site, enhancing brand perception and relevance, and marrying products and services around a cohesive message aligned with their target audience.

The biggest challenge was balancing a comprehensive content strategy with the creation of a limited number of components. We successfully tackled this through organized teamwork and close collaboration between content and design.
cases.outcome: Delivered a cohesive website that functions as a digital brochure, lead generation engine, and customer resource — strengthening Blink's brand recognition and relevance across their global footprint.

metrics.label: Footprint covered by the new site

cases.title: RIMAC's Wellbeing Value Offer
cases.problem: RIMAC, Peru's largest insurance company, tasked us with exploring opportunities to enhance their service ecosystem by offering a cohesive wellbeing solution to complement their insurance services — providing users with personalized experiences that promote healthier lifestyles and offer unique, relevant, and trustworthy coverage.
cases.process:
We executed a five-stage process: Planning (coordinating activities and gathering client context), Discovery (research, interviews, and situation diagnosis), Vision (proposing a direction based on state of the art, contextual factors, and org objectives), Foundations (establishing design systems and user flows), and Design (developing two flows and a validated prototype).

The project's tight timeframe and technical constraints were addressed by creatively leveraging existing solutions within RIMAC's ecosystem — connecting them rather than building from scratch. Tools included journey mapping, user archetypes, stakeholder interviews, workshops, benchmarking, heuristic evaluations, usability testing, and design systems.
cases.outcome: We developed a Wellbeing hub within the RIMAC app, integrating existing services to improve user wellbeing and prevent chronic diseases — creating a seamless extension of their insurance offering that felt personal, trustworthy, and relevant.

cases.title: UX/UI Exploration: Natively Integrated AI for HP
cases.problem: At the dawn of the AI boom, we set out to define how an AI agent should feel when it lives natively within the hardware. This project was not about using AI — it was about designing the interface of its soul. The challenge: making the invisible tangible, secure, and deeply integrated into the HP ecosystem.
cases.process:
We explored a dual-layer integration system: a seamless UI that allows the agent to "reach into" existing HP software and third-party creative suites, and an Integration Marketplace — a modular hub where users plug specific AI capabilities into their workflow through a cohesive iconographic system that feels like a natural OS extension.

We also developed Privacy as a Visual State — two distinct modes so users always know where their data resides. Local Mode (The Vault) uses cooler tones and containment UI for on-device processing. Web Mode (The Horizon) shifts to a warmer, expansive aesthetic when connecting to the cloud, maintaining a clear "connected" status.
cases.outcome:
We moved beyond the "prompt box." By grounding AI in the machine's physical and digital architecture, we transformed a tool into a teammate — ensuring security is felt, not just promised.

We explored a multi-agent architecture with specialized personas: The Researcher (data synthesis and citations), The Mechanic (machine health, thermals, background tasks), and The Librarian (visual indexer for local file retrieval and semantic search).

cases.cardTitle: Understanding how people work with tools
cases.title: Understanding how people work with tools
cases.problem: I was tasked with designing an app to control a torque wrench tool via Bluetooth, despite having limited knowledge about the tool itself. The challenge included understanding torque logic, while lacking access to users or technical experts for insights. Additionally, the same app needed to accommodate three different brands simultaneously.
cases.process: With no UX research available and an ongoing project timeline, we worked directly with technical experts to rapidly understand the domain. We iterated quickly on designs based on their feedback, balancing the constraints of a live project with the need to design coherent experiences across three distinct brand identities — all within the same app architecture.
cases.outcome: We completed the design for all three apps on schedule. The smart wrench was simultaneously prototyped and tested. Today, all three apps are available on the market.

metrics.label: All 3 apps live on the market
